meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no portfolio (Advanced Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no portfolio. A Advanced-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to leverage SaaS pricing strategy to overcome a lack of portfolio. An advanced tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code for fast delivery.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap is a perception issue, not a capability issue. By architecting a high-value SaaS pricing model, you shift the client conversation from 'show me your past work' to 'show me the ROI of your solution.' Deploy this framework to demonstrate business maturity and technical authority immediately.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a portfolio is the freelancer's greatest silent enemy. You have the skills, but the lack of 'social proof' turns high-ticket prospects into skeptics. You don't need a decade of case studies; you need a razor-sharp pricing strategy that signals you understand the economics of their business better than they do.

This tutorial flips the script. We will use Antigravity to scaffold a robust SaaS pricing engine and Claude Code to iterate at the speed of thought. By building a sophisticated pricing calculator as your 'portfolio piece,' you demonstrate that you aren't just a coder—you are a partner who understands how to turn software into sustainable revenue.

steps.title: Define Value-Based Tiers
steps.description: Establish a pricing architecture that maps feature sets to specific business outcomes. Focus on 'Per-Seat' vs 'Usage-Based' logic to show clients you understand scaling.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a SaaS pricing schema with three tiers: Starter, Professional, and Enterprise. Include logic for usage-based billing thresholds and annual discount calculations.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create a pricing_schema.json with validation for the defined tiers'

steps.title: Architect the Calculation Engine
steps.description: Build a robust backend engine that handles currency conversion and tax logic. This shows clients your code is 'production-ready' for global markets.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a TypeScript function that calculates monthly recurring revenue (MRR) based on selected tier, seat count, and an optional 20% annual discount flag.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add ./src/pricingEngine.ts --refactor 'Ensure high precision for currency math'

steps.title: Build the Interactive UI
steps.description: Create a visually compelling pricing table. A clean, responsive UI proves you care about the end-user experience, a key selling point for SaaS founders.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a React pricing table component with hover effects, active state highlighting, and a smooth toggle for annual/monthly billing.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'npm install framer-motion' && claude run 'Implement the pricing component'

steps.title: Integrate Stripe-Ready Logic
steps.description: Prepare your code for real-world payment processing. This signals to clients that your work is ready to take live transactions immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Stripe checkout session handler that maps our internal pricing tiers to Stripe Price IDs and metadata.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add ./src/stripeHandler.ts --verify 'Ensure all tier metadata is passed to Stripe'

steps.title: Stress Test the Architecture
steps.description: Use automated testing to ensure your pricing logic holds up under edge cases. This builds immense trust with non-technical stakeholders.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write Jest unit tests for the pricing engine covering edge cases like zero-seat counts, negative discount inputs, and currency boundary conditions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test --run 'Run all pricing engine tests to verify mathematical accuracy'

pro_tips: Always present three tiers: the 'Anchor' (expensive), the 'Target' (mid-range), and the 'Entry' (low-cost). Most clients will choose the middle, which you have optimized for margin.
Use 'Value Metric' pricing (e.g., price per gigabyte or per lead) instead of flat fees to show you understand how to scale with the client's success.
When showcasing this as a portfolio piece, include a 'Why this works' PDF that explains the conversion rate psychology behind your pricing model.
Hard-code as little as possible. Use environment variables for all pricing constants so the client can change their prices without touching your code.
Always include a 'Contact Sales' CTA for the Enterprise tier to signal that your app is built to handle high-value, custom-negotiated contracts.
content.next_steps: Deploy your pricing engine to a staging domain and add it to your LinkedIn Featured section as 'SaaS Pricing Infrastructure Project'.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to prove your worth and the strategic framework to command higher rates. Don't let a lack of history stop you from building the future. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
